topic.what: ability of SOC analysts to explain their triage decisions for security alarms
topic.how: controlled field experiment with 12 analysts triaging 6 real-world cases and providing open-text justifications
topic.why_matters: SOC operations require transparent decision-making for auditability, incident reporting, and customer justification

characteristics.what_it_measures: correct classification of alarms as true or false positives
characteristics.context: classification was correct but explanation often missing or incorrect

characteristics.what_it_measures: technical correctness of justifications for classification decisions
characteristics.context: major disconnect between making right call and articulating why

characteristics.what_it_measures: number of SOC analysts in field study
characteristics.vs_baseline: vs related work (n=4 in closest study)
characteristics.context: typical for SOC field studies due to access constraints

characteristics.what_it_measures: range of classification accuracy across different alarm types
characteristics.evidence: binomial test p<.05 shows non-random results
characteristics.context: indicates systematic difficulty differences across alarm types

characteristics.what_it_measures: number of security events across study cases
characteristics.evidence: over 30k events distributed across six cases

characteristics.what_it_measures: proportion of explanations that were correct precise or showed technical understanding
characteristics.evidence: 22/72 had correct classification and correct explanation
characteristics.context: 31% correct both ways vs 48% correct classification but wrong explanation

novelty: empirical evidence of disconnect between classification and explanation in SOC
systematic literature review of 257 SOC papers with focus on explanation
real-world field study with production data from operational SOC
qualitative coding approach for open-text justification assessment
open_problems: do findings generalize to other SOCs with different maturity
can AI assistance improve explanation quality without reducing analyst agency
how to design tools that improve information presentation without overwhelming analysts
can regular explanation exercises improve analyst communication skills
